title: Wire BLE NUS egress for bidirectional T-Deck LCI
description: Complete the BLE NUS SLIP local-client path by routing outbound IPv6 packets and CoAP replies back through ble_uart_send_slip() instead of only accepting BLE-originated ingress. Current code has ble_uart_send_slip(), but no caller, so T-Deck must not advertise CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_BLE_LOCAL until this is implemented and smoke-tested. Acceptance: bidirectional packet path test or smoke evidence, generated config with CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_BLE_LOCAL=y only after egress is wired, and app/client notes for UUIDs and framing.
notes:
No-hardware BLE LCI egress implementation evidence (2026-07-01):
- Implemented BLE LCI Zephyr DUMMY_L2 net_if (`ble_lci_netif`) and routed BLE SLIP ingress through `ble_ingress_ipv6(ble_lci_netif_get(), ...)` so ICMPv6/CoAP replies leave via `ble_uart_send_slip()`.
- Production BLE LCI iface now sets `NET_IF_POINTOPOINT` and `NET_IF_IPV6_NO_ND`, brings iface up at init, assigns a deterministic link-local address from the BLE LCI EUI-64, uses per-context TX buffer + mutex instead of a 1280-byte stack buffer, and leaves packet ownership to Zephyr DUMMY_L2.
- Gateway Kconfig now selects `NET_L2_DUMMY` and defaults `CONFIG_NET_IF_MAX_IPV6_COUNT=2` when `CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE=y`.
- Tests extended: `ble_ingress` now compiles production `ble_lci_netif.c`, stubs `ble_uart_send_slip()`, verifies BLE LCI iface point-to-point/no-ND/up state, verifies an injected ICMPv6 Echo Request on BLE LCI exits through BLE SLIP as an Echo Reply, removes pre-existing DUMMY_L2 double-unref in the test send callback, and protects BLE UART stub state with a mutex.

Independent code-review outcomes:
- Banach: P1 no point-to-point/no-ND; P2 1280-byte stack buffer; P2 test double-unref. Fixed by iface flags, per-context TX buffer/mutex, and removing test unref.
- Volta: P1 production IPv6 iface count still 1; P2 test manually created production precondition; P2 test double-unref; P3 qemu-only egress scenario. Fixed by Kconfig default count=2, production iface up/link-local configuration plus test assertion, and double-unref removal. qemu-only nature recorded as expected because `ble_ingress` platform_allow is qemu_x86.
- Wegener: P2 production iface not explicitly up/addressed; P3 brittle cleanup; P3 unlocked stub state. Fixed by production iface up/link-local config, per-test after hook restoring address, and stub mutex.

Validation on EC2 instance i-05e27e074db786e22 using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58, fresh validation worktree `/mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-ble-egress-v4`:
- `git diff --check`: PASS locally before sync.
- `west twister -T lichen/tests/ble_app_owner -T lichen/tests/ble_ingress -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --integration --outdir twister-out-ble-egress-v4 -x=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen`: PASS. 6 scenarios / 11 instances selected, 5 skipped by static filter, 6/11 passed, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings, 62 cases executed, 82 skipped, 270.15s.
- `west build -b nrf52840dk/nrf52840 -d build-ble-egress-v4-nrfdk lichen/apps/gateway -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen`: PASS. FLASH 197376 B / 1 MB (18.82%); RAM 63792 B / 256 KB (24.33%). Generated config: `CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_BLE=y`, `CONFIG_NET_IF_MAX_IPV6_COUNT=2`, `CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_BLE_LOCAL=y`, `CONFIG_NET_L2_DUMMY=y`.
- `west build -b t_echo -d build-ble-egress-v4-techo lichen/apps/gateway -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen`: PASS. FLASH 239096 B / 1 MB (22.80%); RAM 69636 B / 256 KB (26.56%). Generated config has BLE/local/dummy/count=2. Notable existing warnings: duplicate unit-address devicetree warnings and `hal.c:313 return_device_if_ready defined but not used`.
- `west build -b r1_neo -d build-ble-egress-v4-r1neo lichen/apps/gateway -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen`: PASS. FLASH 250276 B / 1 MB (23.87%); RAM 77380 B / 256 KB (29.52%). Generated config has BLE/local/dummy/count=2. Notable existing warnings: duplicate unit-address devicetree warnings and `USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL forced to LOG_LEVEL_NONE`.

Remaining hardware-only evidence is covered by dependent Bead project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.2.4: physical T-Deck BLE central scan/connect, ATT MTU, bidirectional SLIP packet exchange, disconnect cleanup, and HAL/local capability confirmation on actual hardware.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware BLE LCI egress: production BLE LCI netif routes outbound IPv6 to SLIP, focused Twister passed, nRF board builds passed with BLE/local/dummy/count=2 configs. Physical T-Deck validation remains in project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.2.4.
